void-black.$description: Void Black — Dominant page canvas and deepest shadow color — the ground state everything floats above

deep-charcoal.$description: Deep Charcoal — Primary card and section backgrounds; the first surface level above canvas

graphite-700.$description: Graphite 700 — Secondary surface and elevated card backgrounds

graphite-600.$description: Graphite 600 — Observed in other boxShadow, link boxShadow, badge backgroundColor. Extracted usage does not support a distinct primary control color.

graphite-500.$description: Graphite 500 — Border color for dividers, shadow tones on elevated components

graphite-400.$description: Graphite 400 — Subtle borders, muted button borders, body divider lines

slate-300.$description: Slate 300 — Secondary body text, icon fills, disabled states

slate-200.$description: Slate 200 — Tertiary text, muted links, placeholder-level labels

ash-50.$description: Ash 50 — Primary download/CTA button background — warm near-white on black for maximum contrast without pure white aggression

snow.$description: Snow — Primary text on dark surfaces, nav links, headline text, icon strokes, border highlights

ember-red.$description: Ember Red — Observed in body borderColor, other backgroundColor, other fill.

ember-dark.$description: Ember Dark — Observed in other backgroundColor, other borderColor.

mint-signal.$description: Mint Signal — Observed in other backgroundColor. Semantic/state role was not supported by extracted badge/input evidence.

sky-signal.$description: Sky Signal — Observed in other backgroundColor. Semantic/state role was not supported by extracted badge/input evidence.; Hero gradient highlight — linear-gradient(135deg, #56c2ff 0%, #138af2 100%) used for product feature illustration accents

nebula-glow.$description: Nebula Glow — Radial section atmosphere gradient — low-opacity blue bloom behind hero and feature sections

violet-haze.$description: Violet Haze — Radial section atmosphere gradient — low-opacity purple bloom for alternate feature sections

inter.$description: Universal UI font covering everything from 11px badge labels to 64px hero headlines. The negative tracking at display sizes (-0.11em at 56px) is the anti-convention choice — most launcher/productivity tools use neutral tracking, but Raycast's headlines contract inward, creating pressure and density. Feature settings 'ss03' (alternate 'a') distinguish it from default Inter. At small sizes (11–13px), tracking goes strongly positive (up to +0.073em) making metadata scannable at tiny scales.

geistmono.$description: Monospaced font for version strings (v1.104.14), code snippets, homebrew install commands, and keyboard shortcut labels. Weight 300 at 10px for ultra-minimal metadata; weight 500 at 14px for readable code. Positive tracking (+0.017em to +0.05em) keeps characters from colliding at small sizes.

sf-pro-text.$description: System font fallback appearing in macOS-rendered product screenshots and simulated app UI within the page. Not a deliberate web font choice — appears in contexts where the browser renders macOS system UI.

sf-pro.$description: SF Pro — detected in extracted data but not described by AI
